dmc channel mixing test

1. Should play short tone.

2. Should be nearly silent.

3. Should play short tone.

Some clicking might occur between the two tones.Also, a faint tone might be audible through headphones.

noise channel mixing test

1. Should play short tone.

2. Should fade noise in,
and out, without any tone.

3. Should play short tone.

Some clicking might occur between the two tones.Also, a faint tone might be audible through headphones.

square channel mixing test

1. Should play short tone.

2. Should be nearly silent.

3. Should play short tone.

Some clicking might occur between the two tones.Also, a faint tone might be audible through headphones.

triangle channel mixing test

1. Should play short tone.

2. Should be nearly silent.

3. Should play short tone.

Some clicking might occur between the two tones.Also, a faint tone might be audible through headphones.

[0;37mTEST: cpu_dummy_writes_oam
 [0;33mThis program verifies that the
CPU does 2x writes properly.
Any read-modify-write opcode
should first write the origi-
nal value; then the calculated
value exactly 1 cycle later.

Requirement: OAM memory reads
MUST be reliable. This is
often the case on emulators,
but NOT on the real NES.
Nevertheless, this test can be
used to see if the CPU in the
emulator is built properly.
